solution:
def backspaceCompare(s: str, t: str) -> bool:
    s_length, t_length = len(s), len(t)
    for i in range(s_length):
        if s[i] == '#' and t[i] == '#':
            s = s[:i] + s[i+1:]
            t = t[:i] + t[i+1:]
            i += 1
    return s == t

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The algorithm iterates through the input strings character by character. When a '#' character is encountered, it removes the matched characters from both strings and continues the iteration. This ensures that the strings are compared without considering the matched '#' characters. The time complexity is O(n) because the algorithm iterates through the strings once. The space complexity is O(1) because the algorithm only uses a constant amount of extra space for the substrings. The energy implications are low because the algorithm does not involve any resource-intensive operations.
